mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows, directly increasing present valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns during rate cuts are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate exceeding 5.0 percent signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer spending, which reduces corporate earnings expectations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 65% of triggered instances despite the negative signal, or out-of-sample directional accuracy falls below 55% over a 5-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in real sales and housing starts indicate synchronized expansion in consumer and housing demand, which amplifies corporate revenue growth and produces positive quarterly S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: Joint positive MoM moves in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict positive 60-day returns with a directional accuracy above 58%, or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample regression.

mechanism_narrative: Positive month-over-month growth in industrial production during monetary easing cycles reflects accommodative liquidity translating into near-term output expansion, generating positive short-term equity momentum over the next week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns following positive INDPRO MoM growth are not significantly higher than baseline during easing periods, or Rank IC drops below 0.01 in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly CPI inflation exceeding 0.4 percent triggers market expectations of imminent central bank tightening, which compresses equity valuation multiples and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns remain positive in > 60% of high-inflation trigger months, or the signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.03) when CPIAUCSL MoM exceeds 0.4 percent.

mechanism_narrative: A divergence where payroll employment rises while the unemployment rate falls confirms robust labor market tightening and wage growth, which supports corporate earnings expansion and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint PAYEMS/UNRATE divergence signal fails to achieve a directional accuracy above 57% over a 5-year out-of-sample window, or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly GDP growth exceeding the long-term trend reflects sustained aggregate demand expansion, which directly translates into higher corporate profit margins and generates positive quarterly S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns following above-trend GDP prints are not significantly higher than baseline, or the signal's Rank IC remains below 0.02 across all rolling 3-year evaluation windows.

mechanism_narrative: Negative month-over-month industrial production signals supply-side contraction and inventory drawdowns, which forecasts near-term earnings misses and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 65% of negative INDPRO MoM instances, or the negative signal fails to maintain a directional accuracy above 55% in walk-forward testing.
